stories.headline: Nato should let Ukraine join to ‘make all of us stronger’, says Zelenskyy

stories.headline: Trump renews call for US to take over Greenland as he arrives for Nato summit
stories.summary: President also threatens to pull all American troops from Europe and rails against Nato stance on Iran war Europe live – latest updatesDonald Trump has revived his bid for the US to acquire Greenland, threatening to pull all American armed forces out of Europe after the continent repeatedly pushed back.Arriving at the Nato summit in Ankara on Tuesday, the US president also suggested his commitment to defending Europe had been tempered by politica

stories.headline: Le Pen says she will appeal against conviction and run for French presidency – Europe live as it happened
stories.summary: Far-right leader gives TV interview saying that she will appeal against part of today’s ruling which cast doubt on a presidential run The opening speeches are now under way in Ankara, and you can watch them below.This is the Day 1 industry event, not the leaders’ summit, mind you. Continue reading...

stories.headline: What does the court ruling mean for Marine Le Pen’s presidential chances?
stories.summary: A look at the the case, the appeal court decision, and how it could affect the race to succeed Emmanuel MacronMarine Le Pen has said she will be a candidate in France’s presidential race next year, after a court ruling on her appeal against a conviction for embezzlement of public funds allowed her to run.However, the path ahead is far from straightforward. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Zelenskyy renews call for Ukraine's NATO membership citing military might ahead of summit
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President has renewed his push to join NATO, insisting his country has already proved itself a reliable partner and that its membership in the alliance would be “entirely natural”.

stories.headline: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
stories.summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen found guilty, but court clears way for presidential run if she wears tag
stories.summary: The National Rally leader now has to decide whether she will run with an electronic tag and will speak on TV later.

stories.headline: Two mothers, each certain a viral Gaza prisoner photo shows her son
stories.summary: Two Palestinian mothers believe a viral image of a bound Gaza detainee shows their missing son.

stories.headline: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president
stories.summary: Politician sets up unprecedented election campaign next year after judges uphold her conviction for embezzlement

stories.headline: France's Marine Le Pen, who brought hard-right National Rally to cusp of electoral success
stories.summary: She has tasted defeat before, but if the appeal court bars her from running in the 2027 presidential election, it could end her political career.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race
stories.summary: The Maine Democrat has faced an escalating string of scandals as he competes for a critical Senate seat that could determine control of the chamber.

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: France’s Marine Le Pen must wear ankle tag after appeal ruling
stories.summary: A French appeals court upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing European Parliament funds.

stories.headline: French far-right leader Le Pen says will run in 2027 presidential election
stories.summary: Her announcement followed a court ruling that reduced the length of time she was banned from holding public office.

stories.headline: China missile test over Pacific alarms island nations
Marine Le Pen vows to run in France’s 2027 presidential election despite fraud conviction
stories.summary: The leader of the National Rally announced on French TV that she would remain a candidate for the 2027 presidential election despite a court finding her guilty of embezzling EU funds.

stories.headline: Nigel Farage says he's resigning as MP and will run in 'people vs. the establishment' by-election
stories.summary: "I've decided that the people of Clacton should be the judges of my actions," Farage said. "It's a chance to stick two fingers up to the entire establishment".

stories.headline: Fatal Typhoon Maysak floods burst dam wall in China
stories.summary: At least four have been killed by flooding due to Maysak, while another 62,000 have been evacuated across the southern Guangxi province.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Calls on Graham Platner to Drop Senate Bid After Rape Allegation
stories.summary: Mr. Sanders was one of the last remaining prominent allies of Graham Platner, the Maine Senate Democratic nominee, to call for him to drop out after a rape allegation.

stories.headline: Abandoned by Allies, Graham Platner Faces Pressure to End Maine Senate Campaign
stories.summary: After Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee in Maine, was accused of rape, much of the party and several key supporters turned against him.

stories.headline: Democratic factions vie to pick Platner replacement before he leaves Maine race
stories.summary: Potential candidates and their backers moved quickly to gain an edge in the contest over who would replace Graham Platner if he exits the key Senate race.

stories.headline: Tokyo's property boom is a warning, not a crisis

stories.summary: The lawyer for Dr Hussam Abu Safiya tells the BBC his client was so badly beaten he could not recognise him during a visit last week.

stories.headline: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
stories.summary: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

stories.headline: Lawsuit Alleges Trump Administration Gave Iran Details on Asylum Seekers
stories.summary: A new lawsuit filed in the District of Columbia states that the administration allowed Iranian officials to “select” which Iranians seeking refuge in the United States would be expelled.

stories.headline: U.S. gave Tehran details on Iranian asylum seekers, lawsuit alleges
stories.summary: The claim, outlined in court papers, contends information shared with Iran could jeopardize the lives of pro-democracy protesters, religious minorities and LGTBQ people.

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.headline: Trump says will lift sanctions on Turkiye, ‘consider’ selling F-35s
stories.summary: Announcement during Ankara meeting suggests pivot in US policy, despite opposition from Israel.

stories.headline: India's landmark Ram temple overhauls board after alleged theft of donations
stories.summary: Questions have been raised over alleged theft of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.headline: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
stories.summary: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

stories.headline: US Revokes Waiver Allowing Iran Oil Sales After Attacks
stories.summary: The US Treasury Department revoked a waiver that allowed the sale of Iranian oil in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, jeopardizing an interim peace deal between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: Chile’s New Pension Rules Spark Industry Divide on Effects
stories.summary: Analysts, strategists and investors are struggling to weigh the impact — if any — a new investment regime for Chile’s pension funds will have on the broader market.

stories.headline: Italy Sells $6 Billion in First Dollar-Bond Sale Since 2021
stories.summary: Italy returned to bond market for the first time since the pandemic, broadening its pool of foreign investors after the country’s improving finances won credit-rating upgrades.

stories.headline: Andrea Shaw, Plaintiff in Anti-Vaccine Suit, Is Charged With Murder of Her Twins
stories.summary: A lawsuit filed by Children’s Health Defense, an organization founded by Robert F. Kennedy Jr., claims that vaccines caused the twins’ deaths. But a grand jury in Idaho has indicted her on charges of suffocating them.

stories.headline: A Supreme Court Case on AR-15s Could Hinge on Their ‘Common Use’
stories.summary: Last week, the Supreme Court took up challenges to two assault weapons bans. Its decision could seriously limit the government’s ability to ban certain guns.

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. stories.summary: Musk-led company joined the index on Tuesday, less than a month after its stock market debut on June 12.

stories.headline: US trade deficit surges amid artificial intelligence spending boom

Morocco multiplies its olive oil sales in Spain by 100: is it taking over the market?

stories.headline: EU still can't prove €43 billion in home renovation funds actually saved energy
stories.summary: The EU accelerated home renovations after the pandemic, but according to its own auditors, it still lacks convincing evidence that the program delivered the energy savings and value for taxpayers that justified its massive investment.

stories.headline: ECB tells Europe's biggest banks to prepare for AI-powered cyber threats
stories.summary: The European Central Bank has given the eurozone's largest lenders until the end of October to explain how they will strengthen their cyber defences against increasingly capable AI systems.

stories.headline: Euronews launches Kazakh language broadcasting in Astana
stories.summary: As part of its continued development in Central Asia, Euronews now takes the strategic step to launch its new Kazakh language service and bring its European-perspective journalism and global coverage to Kazakhstan.

stories.headline: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.headline: China tests missile in the Pacific hours after Australia-Fiji alliance signed
stories.summary: The test launch came hours after Australia signed a new defence pact with its Pacific island neighbour Fiji.

stories.headline: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
stories.summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

stories.headline: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: After America’s 250th, Trump will test how far he can push NATO allies
stories.summary: As NATO leaders gather in Turkey, they are preparing for another summit shaped as much by managing President Donald Trump as by the alliance's security agenda.

stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

stories.headline: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.headline: Chip Stocks Sink After Blistering Run as Oil Jumps: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Wall Street was rattled by a selloff in chipmakers on concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations while a jump in oil prices boosted bond yields.

stories.headline: Oil Jumps After Ship Strikes Spur US to Revoke Iran Sales Waiver
stories.summary: Oil climbed almost 6% after attacks on Persian Gulf shipping prompted the US to revoke a waiver that had relaxed restrictions on Iranian crude sales.

stories.headline: Gold Slumps as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path After Iran Oil Waiver
stories.summary: Gold slumped after the US Treasury Department revoked a waiver allowing the sale of Iranian oil, stoking concern that elevated energy prices will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

stories.headline: Sports-Related Deals on the Rise: William Blair
stories.summary: Acquisitions in the sports industry are set to increase as investors leverage a slew of different approaches to the fast-growing sector, according to William Blair & Co.’s head of investment banking.

stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes

stories.headline: Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
stories.summary: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Iran nuclear and military damage revealed after restricted satellite images released
stories.summary: Targets hit include ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation

stories.headline: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas

stories.headline: Donald Trump’s AI regime is opaque, unpredictable—and unsustainable
